reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: NO-GO theorem (cross-model verified): the two-auxiliary + one-edge height-control escape CANNOT prove the fresh-1/b lemma. The integer-forcing T=1 forces the FIXED hyperbola (q1-C1)(q2-C2)=C1*C2+b, so for fixed b only finitely many auxiliary primes exist (bounded by C_max^2+C_max+b) — an adversarial F containing them all kills the certificate. One-prime AND two-aux inductions are both dead.
depends_on: OPEN: fresh-1/b lemma needs an UNBOUNDED (grows-with-F) cone construction; all fixed-topology approaches (k=1, k=2) provably fail. k>=3 circular system doesn't collapse to a hyperbola but a fixed topology still has finitely many solutions.
attempt.detail: Verified: a 2-aux+1-edge cert for 1/b satisfies (q1-C1)(q2-C2)=N:=C1*C2+b (RHS fixed => q_i-C_i are divisors of N => finitely many pairs). Confirmed on 1/210: both known solutions (157,307) and (31,7867) give (q1-30)(q2-247)=7620=30*247+210, T=1. The largest-prime-factor escape DOES give a fresh q2 (LC2) with T<2, but LC1 then collapses to the fixed hyperbola — verified empirically (b=210: of 60k q1, fresh+T<2 held 5501x, BOTH local congruences held 1x). STRUCTURAL WARNING (proven): for ANY fixed b,k,topology,subsets, T=1 has only finitely many prime solutions (large q_j => RHS<1 => some q_j bounded). So a fresh-universal proof CANNOT use a fixed finite topology — the cone graph SIZE must GROW with F. NEXT TARGET: a growing-cycle / expander local-congruence lemma (graph size grows with the forbidden set). #306 remains OPEN; the bounded-construction routes are dead.
attempt.insight: independent Opus verification: hyperbola identity on both 1/210 solutions (T=1); empirical M(q1) scan to 60k (both-locals = 1).

provenance.proposer: GPT-5.5 Pro (no-go round), verified by Opus

attempt.reusable_for: integer-forcing cone certificates; fixed-hyperbola no-go for 2-variable sum-of-unit-fractions; why bounded gadget topologies can't be fresh-universal
